He never laughs; he rarely even smiles, and then only on his son: his countenance ordinarily bearing a look of bitterness tempered by affection, while his general expression is one of caressing tenderness. [OC-009-024]During the ensuing days the distance between the two planets continued to decrease, and it became more and more obvious that the earth, on her new orbit, was about to cross the orbit of Venus. Throughout this time the earth had been making a perceptible approach towards Mercury, and that planet--which is rarely visible to the naked eye, and then only at what are termed the periods of its greatest eastern and western elongations--now appeared in all its splendor. It amply justified the epithet of "sparkling" which the ancients were accustomed to confer upon it, and could scarcely fail to awaken a new interest. The periodic recurrence of its phases; its reflection of the sun's rays, shedding upon it a light and a heat seven times greater than that received by the earth; its glacial and its torrid zones, which, on account of the great inclination of the axis, are scarcely separable; its equatorial bands; its mountains eleven miles high;--were all subjects of observation worthy of the most studious regard.

Behring Strait is the real passage between Asia and America that governs the fate of the drifting island in *The Fur Country*. It is the colony's hoped-for lifeline, the route by which "the vessels from Behring Strait to come right up to the fort, bringing us fresh provisions and taking away our commodities." (FC-011-006)
Aronnax dreads it — "The Torres Strait is regarded as no less dangerous for its bristling reefs than for the savage inhabitants of its coasts." (TTLU-020-021)

The archipelago is two principal isles split by a strait: "On the deep waters of the Falkland Strait, which separates the two principal isles, great masses of extraordinary aquatic vegetation floated." (AM-009-021)
# Melville Island

Washburn Bay is a deep inlet of the northern Canadian coast, one of the landmarks charted by Lieutenant Jaspar Hobson's fur-trade survey in *The Fur Country*. The expedition first rounds it early in its march: "On the 4th July the travellers skirted round another deep bay called Washburn Bay, and reached the furthest point of a little lake." (FC-012-019)
description: 'Rock at the mouth of the Mediterranean, a surviving fragment carried off by the comet Gallia.'

Australia is the second great landfall in the hunt for Captain Grant, taken up when the reading of the document is revised. Glenarvan is challenged to contend, with the document in his hand, that the shipwreck of the Britannia happened on the shores of Australia. (SC-024-010) On that reading the whole expedition turns south.
